acceptance_targets: [CH08-BOOKER-001] PASS if the revised equivalents of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:183-292 include at least one external obstacle that changes the transfer plan and requires an explicit corrective action on-page; FAIL if money still moves from authorization to delivery without interruption or adaptation.

[CH08-CRAFT-001] PASS if one revised scene anchored to the current snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:289 region includes direct interaction with either the Ryu or Pak household, containing at least one line of recipient dialogue and one specific physical/economic detail (e.g., bill receipt, medication access, rent notice). FAIL if relief remains reported only via administrative summary or relay speech.

[CH08-CRAFT-002] PASS if within the revised equivalents of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:261-314, at least one concrete negative consequence appears before the chapter closes and changes Ina's next tactical choice; FAIL if the only consequence remains a ledger note that risk was accepted.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised equivalents of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:183-292 include at least one external obstacle that changes the transfer plan and requires an explicit corrective action on-page; FAIL if money still moves from authorization to delivery without interruption or adaptation.
findings.problem: After Ina decides the formal channel is closed, the fund-diversion plotline resolves with too little active resistance. The operation moves from decision to disbursement mostly cleanly, which weakens chapter-level suspense and consequence density for a high-risk illegal act.
findings.rewrite_direction: Strengthen causal resistance in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:183 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:261 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:289 by inserting at least one concrete obstacle (internal audit flag, intermediary refusal, or timing failure) that forces Ina/Hyejin to adapt strategy under pressure before funds land.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if one revised scene anchored to the current snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:289 region includes direct interaction with either the Ryu or Pak household, containing at least one line of recipient dialogue and one specific physical/economic detail (e.g., bill receipt, medication access, rent notice). FAIL if relief remains reported only via administrative summary or relay speech.
findings.problem: Sanitization drift: the chapter frames civilian harm and restitution primarily through ledger entries and secondhand reporting. At the exact moral-pressure point (relief delivery), the prose summarizes outcomes instead of rendering embodied contact with harmed people.
findings.rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:27 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:289, replace at least one summary-only harm/relief report with an on-page encounter (in-person or live call) that shows concrete setting, body response, and material consequence for a named household.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if within the revised equivalents of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:261-314, at least one concrete negative consequence appears before the chapter closes and changes Ina's next tactical choice; FAIL if the only consequence remains a ledger note that risk was accepted.
findings.problem: Timidity in consequence rendering: Ina acknowledges embezzlement risk, but the immediate emotional and operational aftershock is muted. The prose retreats into controlled notation quickly, reducing the felt danger established by the premise.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten consequence presence around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:261 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:303 by adding an immediate downstream cost (audit notification, compromised contact, panic physiology that impairs next step, or forced contingency move) linked directly to the illegal transfer.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into uncomfortable specificity when stakes demand it: show coercion mechanics, sexual negotiation, grief rituals, and financial desperation at scene level. In edge cases, prefer precision over decorum; protect character dignity through perspective accuracy, not by omitting the hard material.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene requires bodily, economic, or moral consequence and the draft retreats into abstract phrasing, rewrite with concrete action, sensation, and decision impact before proceeding.
aesthetic_risk_policy.operational_resolution_rule: When uncertain between tasteful distance and narrative truth, choose the version that preserves causal accountability and embodied consequence, then tune language for clarity rather than softness.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the baseline in spoken dialogue; uncontracted forms are reserved for deliberate emphasis, legal language, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter structures deliberately: high-density dialogue vs interior reflection, multi-scene public collision vs two-scene intimate pressure, aftermath inventory vs anticipatory dread. Never let two consecutive chapters share the same pacing architecture or turn mechanism.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, unsentimental literary diction that permits technical vocabulary, profanity, and class-marked speech without flattening idiolects.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person centered on Seo Ina, with brief controlled widenings in public-event chapters.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternating pressure waves: compressed clauses in operational stress, longer breath-based lines in grief and reflection.
